note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's <break> tags and sentence boundaries (caption-sized, <=~95 chars). Block windows were measured by FFT cross-correlation of each WC-14-02-sNN.mp3 against the joined master (8 kHz mono PCM, numpy rfft): the Sigma(ffprobe audio+0.4) formula predicts 262.428s but the ffprobed master is 261.956s (0.472s off, past the +/-0.3s gate). Cross-correlation shows the master is the 13 blocks concatenated with a uniform ~0.35-0.37s gap after each; per-file ffprobe durations are inflated ~0.03s each by mp3 encoder-delay padding that the PCM concat strips, and the error accumulates (whole discrepancy). Each block startS is its measured onset (multiples of the 0.08s mp3-frame grid); final end = ffprobed master duration 261.956s. Product/label names are rendered at their true spelling in captions (WashCentral, LMS); the script's 'Wash Central' / 'L-M-S' are TTS pronunciation spellings per the narration's pronunciation guide, so the spoken words are unchanged.

phrases.text: The Academy has a second half.
Click in from the rail and you land on the same learner dashboard as last lesson,
with the Academy running its own sidebar instead of the platform rail.
Today we go past the learner pages, into the group underneath.
Read both sidebar groups first.
The top one holds the learner pages you already know.
Below it sits Admin, four doors:
the dashboard, users and enrollment, course management, and reports.
One naming note.
The learning plan says LMS Administration, the sidebar says Admin,
and the screen always wins.
Open Admin Dashboard.
The subtitle states the job plainly: manage training across your organization.
Up in the header sits an Enroll Users button.
Find it, remember where it lives, and leave it alone.
On this shared tenant, enrolling someone creates a real training record.
Five tiles across the top.
Nineteen learners active this month.
A twenty six percent completion rate across one hundred eighty six enrollments,
with nine hours forty six minutes of average time spent.
And forty nine items overdue, flagged action needed.
That last tile is where every rollout conversation starts.
Scroll to the completion heatmap.
All nineteen courses against all twenty five users.
Take the top row.
Getting Started with WashCentral:
six finished, seven in progress, six overdue, six never began, twenty four percent complete.
One glance per course tells you where a crew's training actually stands.
Three admin actions live on this page.
Enroll Users in the header, then Manage, then New Announcement.
Each one either creates training records or sends a message to real people.
So today they get located and named, never opened for use.
That restraint is half the lesson.
Second door, Users and Enrollment.
Twenty five learners.
Twenty seven percent average completion on required courses,
and seventeen people carrying something overdue.
Fully compliant reads zero.
Nobody on this tenant has finished everything required of them yet,
and that is this page's headline.
Read one row of the table.
Marcus Johnson, a general manager in Fort Lauderdale,
three of seven required courses done, overdue, last active a month ago.
Required progress against status is your per person compliance read.
His row also offers Assign Course.
Not on the demo.
Third door, Course Management.
Three sections stack down the page:
the courses themselves, the learning paths that chain them, and the certifications.
A New Course button and an Import control sit in the toolbar, and both stay untouched.
This is the page where the library gets shaped.
Look at the first course card.
Twenty five enrolled, twenty four percent complete, an average score of seventy eight.
A publish toggle reads On, with four controls beside it: settings, edit, preview, learners.
Flip that toggle and a course disappears for real staff.
So it stays exactly where it is.
Last door, Reports and Compliance.
Seven report tabs run across the top, completion overview through scheduled reports.
The tiles repeat the dashboard's story,
one eighty six enrollments, twenty six percent complete, forty nine overdue,
plus an eighty one percent average quiz score.
When two pages agree on the numbers, you can quote them.
Stay on the overview and scroll.
A year of completions charted, then the two tables a client review runs on.
By location: Fort Lauderdale, five users, forty four enrollments,
twelve complete, thirteen overdue.
By course: Getting Started, twenty five enrolled, six complete,
about two hours of time spent apiece.
The learning plan promised this page, and it is live.
Four pages, four jobs.
The dashboard for the org wide read.
Users and Enrollment for the per person truth.
Course Management for the levers.
Reports and Compliance for the numbers a client review needs.
You reached all four from the sidebar, and you changed nothing along the way.
